# What is Libwebsockets
Libwebsockets is an Open Source C library for Websocket client/server under MIT license. There
are also support for HTTP/1 and HTTP/2.

Docs and intro
https://libwebsockets.org/

Source code on Github
https://github.com/warmcat/libwebsockets

# TLS support

We use BoringSSL for TLS.

# Build Libwebsockets

Libwebsockets uses CMake. A custom BUILD files (BUILD and libwebsockets.BUILD) are created to build
using Bazel. The current build supports MacOS, Android, iOS, and Windows.

To build the lib itself
```
bazel build @libwebsockets//:libwebsockets
```

To build a simple sample
```
bazel build //bzl/examples/libwebsockets:websocket_test
```

## Config headers

Libwebsockets uses two generated headers to configure which features are enabled. The header is
generated by CMake. To build with Bazel, run CMake for target platforms and generate headers and
copy here. Some minor tweaks made to fit with the toolchain we use.

The BUILD file has a file group with select depending on taget platform, which determine to pick
proper headers for targets.

## Source, copts, linkopts

There are some target platform specific sources, copts, and linkopts. See libwebsockets.BUILD.

## Patches

There are two patches
- private-lib-plat-windows.h: We need TLS support but enbling TLS including "crypt.h", which has
conflicting macro definitions with BoringSSL macros and causes compilation errors on Windows. This
Patch adds undefs to avoid macro conflicts. We do not need Windows defined macros but need BoringSSL macros.
- sha-1.c: This has function name conflicts with BoringSSL and the patch changes function name to
avoid symbol conflict.
